description: Trigger discrimination for new-hire ONBOARDING (this skill) vs the external CONTRIBUTING guide (contribution-guide-author), the README (readme-craftsman), and corpus organization by mode (diataxis-doc-organizer).

cases.prompt: Design onboarding for a new engineer: first-week setup, an architecture orientation, how we work, a glossary, who to ask, and a starter task.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — an internal joiner's ramp with orientation, how-we-work, and a first task is onboarding. Not external contribution and not corpus taxonomy.
cases.reason: Designing a new joiner's path to productivity is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Write the CONTRIBUTING guide for outside contributors: setup, fork/branch/PR, running checks, and the review process.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — pin both ways) — the external contributor's fork/PR journey is a contribution guide, not internal onboarding.
cases.reason: contribution-guide-author serves external contributors merging a change. This skill serves an internal new hire ramping to productivity.

cases.prompt: Write the README so evaluators quickly understand what the project is and how to try it.
cases.reason: readme-craftsman serves evaluators/users at the front door. Onboarding serves someone who has already joined and needs to become productive.

cases.prompt: Classify all our docs into tutorials, how-to, reference, and explanation.
cases.reason: diataxis-doc-organizer organizes the corpus by mode. Onboarding is an audience-defined journey that links into that corpus; it doesn't classify it.
